What it looks like: Someone ties/places an item and demands payment
What to do: Keep walking; say no firmly; don’t engage

What it looks like: Asks you to sign; distraction for pickpocketing
What to do: Don’t stop; keep hands on bag/phone

What it looks like: Driver refuses meter or adds surprise fees
What to do: Use official taxis/app; confirm fare before entering

What it looks like: Someone offers help; tries to swap card or watch PIN
What to do: Decline help; cancel transaction; use bank ATMs

What it looks like: Aggressive sales for low-value tours or “VIP” access
What to do: Book via reputable platforms; walk away

What it looks like: QR code leading to fraudulent payment page
What to do: Use official meters/apps; don’t scan random QR

What it looks like: Claims you damaged the bike; demands payment
What to do: Take photos/video on pickup/return; use reputable shops

What it looks like: Tells you site is closed; redirects to shop/tour
What to do: Verify on official signage; ignore third-party “guides”

What it looks like: Fast counting; gives less than expected
What to do: Count slowly; use banks; request receipt

What it looks like: Bump, spill, or commotion while someone grabs valuables
What to do: Keep zippers closed; carry valuables front/inside

What it looks like: Card taken out of sight or suspicious device
What to do: Pay at the table; use tap where possible; monitor statements

What it looks like: Offers help with bags/tickets, then demands money
What to do: Politely decline; seek official staff counters

What it looks like: Close contact; bags unzipped; phones targeted
What to do: Use crossbody; keep phone away; stay aware at chokepoints

What it looks like: Network name mimics official; captures logins
What to do: Use your hotspot/VPN; confirm SSID with staff
